hazards.name: Runaway Exothermic Reaction
hazards.description: Loss of temperature control in a batch reactor during an exothermic synthesis step, leading to thermal runaway with potential for vessel rupture and toxic release.
hazards.topEventDescription: Reactor temperature exceeds the maximum allowable temperature for the reaction, and cooling capacity is insufficient to arrest the temperature rise.

threats.name: Cooling System Failure
threats.description: Loss of cooling water supply, heat exchanger fouling, or coolant valve failure during the exothermic phase of the reaction.

barriers.name: Redundant Cooling Systems
barriers.description: Dual cooling circuits with automatic switchover on loss of flow. Emergency chilled water supply from independent tank with gravity feed.

barriers.name: Pre-Batch Cooling System Verification
barriers.description: Mandatory pre-batch checklist confirming cooling water flow, temperature, pressure, and valve positions before reagent addition begins.

threats.name: Incorrect Reagent Addition Rate
threats.description: Reagent added too quickly or in incorrect quantity, causing heat generation to exceed the cooling system's removal capacity.

barriers.name: Automated Dosing Control
barriers.description: PLC-controlled reagent dosing with flow metering and automatic shutoff. Interlock prevents addition if reactor temperature exceeds setpoint.

barriers.name: Operator Training and Batch Records
barriers.description: Competency-assessed operators follow validated batch manufacturing record. Two-person verification for critical additions.

consequences.name: Reactor Vessel Rupture
consequences.description: Overpressure from thermal runaway exceeds vessel design pressure, causing catastrophic failure and release of reactor contents.

barriers.name: Emergency Relief System
barriers.description: Rupture disc and relief valve sized for runaway scenario per DIERS methodology. Vents to catch tank or quench system, not atmosphere.

barriers.name: Emergency Quench System
barriers.description: Automated injection of kill agent (cold solvent or inhibitor) triggered by high-high temperature alarm. Arrests reaction within 30 seconds.

consequences.name: Toxic Gas Release to Atmosphere
consequences.description: Release of toxic reaction byproducts or decomposition gases affecting plant personnel and potentially the surrounding community.

barriers.name: Scrubber and Abatement System
barriers.description: All reactor vents route through caustic scrubber before atmospheric discharge. Scrubber pH monitored continuously with automatic reagent dosing.

barriers.name: Community Alert System
barriers.description: Automated community warning sirens triggered by confirmed toxic release detection. Pre-agreed shelter-in-place protocol with local authority.
